title: Peru Launches Human Trafficking Investigation into Recruitment of Citizens to Fight for Russia in Ukraine
summary: Peru's public prosecutor opened an investigation into an alleged trafficking network that lured citizens with false job promises to Russia, where they ended up fighting in Ukraine. An estimated 600 Peruvians have reportedly joined Russian forces since October.
contents:
## Peru Investigates Trafficking of Citizens to Russian Front Lines

### Investigation Launch
Peru's public prosecutor's office launched a formal investigation into an alleged human trafficking network that recruited Peruvian citizens under false pretenses to fight for Russia in Ukraine. The investigation focuses on "human trafficking" and "aggravated human trafficking" charges.

### Recruitment Details
- Individuals received recruitment through "deceptive job offers to work as security agents and other roles" in Russia
- Monthly salary promises ranged from $2,000 to $3,000
- An estimated 600 Peruvians have traveled to fight for Russia since October 2025
- 13 Peruvian citizens have reportedly died in the conflict so far
- Moscow's embassy in Lima acknowledged Peruvians signed contracts to join Russian armed forces

### Diplomatic Response
Peru's Ministry of Foreign Affairs asked the Russian embassy to clarify the situation and provide information about the location and wellbeing of Peruvian citizens serving in the Russian military. Under Peruvian law, citizens must obtain Foreign Ministry permission before serving in a foreign military.

### Families Protest
Relatives of victims who claim recruitment under false pretenses protested outside the Foreign Ministry in Lima, demanding repatriation of their loved ones.

### Broader Pattern
Peru joins a growing list of countries raising complaints against Russia over deceptive recruitment of foreign nationals. More than 1,780 citizens from 36 African countries reportedly fight alongside Russian forces, according to Ukrainian estimates. Russia previously acknowledged enlisting thousands of North Korean soldiers.
